name: Druid
description: A priest of the Old Faith, wielding the powers of nature and adopting animal forms to protect the natural world.

features.name: Druidic
features.description:
You know Druidic, the secret language of Druids. While learning this ancient tongue, you also unlocked the magic of communicating with animals; you always have the {spell:Speak with Animals} spell prepared.

You can use Druidic to leave hidden messages. You and others who know Druidic automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message's presence with a successful DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check but can't decipher it without magic.

features.name: Primal Order
features.description:
You have dedicated yourself to one of the following sacred roles of your choice.

**Magician.** You know one extra cantrip from the Druid spell list. In addition, your mystical connection to nature gives you a bonus to your Intelligence (Arcana or Nature) checks equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum bonus of +1).

**Warden.** Trained for battle, you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and training with Medium armor.

properties.text: You know one extra cantrip from the Druid spell list. You gain a bonus to Intelligence (Arcana or Nature) checks equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1).

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description:
You have learned to cast spells through studying the mystical forces of nature. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Druid spells.

**Cantrips.** You know two cantrips of your choice from the Druid spell list. Druidcraft and Produce Flame are recommended. Whenever you gain a Druid level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip from the Druid spell list. When you reach Druid levels 4 and 10, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the Druid spell list.

**Spell Slots.** The Druid Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Prepared Spells of Level 1+.** You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. The number of spells on your list is shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Druid Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Druid spell list. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

**Changing Your Prepared Spells.** Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells with other Druid spells for which you have spell slots.

**Spellcasting Focus.** You can use a Druidic Focus as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus} for your Druid spells.

features.name: Wild Companion
features.description:
You can summon a nature spirit that assumes an animal form to aid you. As a Magic action, you can expend a spell slot or a use of Wild Shape to cast the {spell:Find Familiar} spell without Material components.

When you cast the spell in this way, the familiar is Fey and disappears when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

features.name: Wild Shape
features.description:
The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you shape-shift into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature (see Known Forms below). You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Druid level or until you use Wild Shape again, have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a {rule:Bonus Action}.

**Number of Uses.** You can use Wild Shape twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You gain additional uses when you reach certain Druid levels, as shown in the Wild Shape column of the Druid Features table.

**Known Forms.** You know four Beast forms for this feature, chosen from among Beast stat blocks that have a maximum Challenge Rating of 1/4 and that lack a Fly Speed. The Rat, Riding Horse, Spider, and Wolf are recommended. Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can replace one of your known forms with another eligible form. When you reach certain Druid levels, your number of known forms and the maximum Challenge Rating for those forms increases (level 4: 6 forms, CR 1/2; level 8: 8 forms, CR 1, Fly Speed allowed).

**Rules While Shape-Shifted.** While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak.
- **Temporary Hit Points.** When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Druid level.
- **Game Statistics.** Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast's stat block, but you retain your creature type; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You also retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies and use your Proficiency Bonus for them, in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast's stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.
- **No Spellcasting.** You can't cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn't break your {status:Concentration} or otherwise interfere with a spell you've already cast.
- **Objects.** Your ability to handle objects is determined by the form's limbs rather than your own.

properties.text: When assuming a Wild Shape form, gain Temporary Hit Points equal to Druid level. Can't cast spells while shape-shifted (Concentration is maintained).

features.name: Druid Subclass
features.description: You gain a Druid subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Druid levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Druid level or lower.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Druid levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Wild Resurgence
features.description:
Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required).

In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a level 1 spell slot, but you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Once per turn, if you have no Wild Shape uses, you can expend a spell slot to gain one Wild Shape use (no action required). Once per Long Rest, you can expend one Wild Shape use to gain a level 1 spell slot (no action required).

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Subclass.

features.name: Elemental Fury
features.description:
The might of the elements flows through you. You gain one of the following options of your choice.

**Potent Spellcasting.** Add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

**Primal Strike.** Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).

properties.text: Add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Druid cantrip.

Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form's attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Subclass.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass Feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your Druid Subclass.

features.name: Improved Elemental Fury
features.description:
The option you chose for Elemental Fury grows more powerful.

**Potent Spellcasting.** When you cast a Druid cantrip with a range of 10 feet or greater, the spell's range increases by 300 feet.

**Primal Strike.** The extra damage of your Primal Strike increases to 2d8.

properties.text: Improved Elemental Fury: if Potent Spellcasting, Druid cantrips with range 10+ feet gain +300 feet range; if Primal Strike, extra damage increases to 2d8.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the {feat:Ability Score Improvement} feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Beast Spells
features.description: While using Wild Shape, you can cast spells in Beast form, except for any spell that has a Material component with a cost specified or that consumes its Material component.

properties.text: While in Wild Shape, you can cast Druid spells that don't have a costly or consumed Material component.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Dimensional Travel is recommended.

features.name: Archdruid
features.description:
The vitality of nature constantly blooms within you, granting you the following benefits.

**Evergreen Wild Shape.** Whenever you roll {rule:Initiative} and have no uses of Wild Shape left, you regain one expended use of it.

**Nature Magician.** You can convert uses of Wild Shape into a spell slot (no action required). Choose a number of your unexpended uses of Wild Shape and convert them into a single spell slot, with each use contributing 2 spell levels. For example, if you convert two uses of Wild Shape, you produce a level 4 spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

**Longevity.** The primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every ten years that pass, your body ages only one year.

properties.text: Evergreen Wild Shape: on Initiative roll with no Wild Shape uses, regain one use. Nature Magician: convert Wild Shape uses into a spell slot (each use = 2 spell levels); once per Long Rest. Longevity: age 1 year per 10 years that pass.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Land
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Land comprises mystics and sages who safeguard ancient knowledge and rites. These Druids meet within sacred circles of trees or standing stones to whisper primal secrets in Druidic. The circle's wisest members preside as the chief priests of their communities.

features.name: Circle of the Land Spells
features.description:
Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, choose one type of land: arid, polar, temperate, or tropical. Consult the table below that corresponds to the chosen type; you have the spells listed for your Druid level and lower prepared.

**Arid Land:** Level 3: {spell:Blur}, {spell:Burning Hands}, {spell:Fire Bolt}; Level 5: {spell:Fireball}; Level 7: {spell:Blight}; Level 9: {spell:Wall of Stone}.

**Polar Land:** Level 3: {spell:Fog Cloud}, {spell:Hold Person}, {spell:Ray of Frost}; Level 5: {spell:Sleet Storm}; Level 7: {spell:Ice Storm}; Level 9: {spell:Cone of Cold}.

**Temperate Land:** Level 3: {spell:Misty Step}, {spell:Shocking Grasp}, {spell:Sleep}; Level 5: {spell:Lightning Bolt}; Level 7: {spell:Freedom of Movement}; Level 9: {spell:Tree Stride}.

**Tropical Land:** Level 3: {spell:Acid Splash}, {spell:Ray of Sickness}, {spell:Web}; Level 5: {spell:Stinking Cloud}; Level 7: {spell:Polymorph}; Level 9: {spell:Insect Plague}.

properties.text: On each Long Rest, choose a land type (arid, polar, temperate, or tropical). Gain those spells as always-prepared Druid spells based on Druid level. Spells not on the Druid list still count as Druid spells for you.

options.label: Arid
options.description: You learn Fire Bolt cantrip. Always prepared: Burning Hands, Blur (L3), Fireball (L5), Blight (L7), Wall of Stone (L9).

options.label: Polar
options.description: You learn Ray of Frost cantrip. Always prepared: Fog Cloud, Hold Person (L3), Sleet Storm (L5), Ice Storm (L7), Cone of Cold (L9).

options.label: Temperate
options.description: You learn Shocking Grasp cantrip. Always prepared: Sleep, Misty Step (L3), Lightning Bolt (L5), Freedom of Movement (L7), Tree Stride (L9).

options.label: Tropical
options.description: You learn Acid Splash cantrip. Always prepared: Ray of Sickness, Web (L3), Stinking Cloud (L5), Polymorph (L7), Insect Plague (L9).

features.name: Land's Aid
features.description:
As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of yourself. Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear for a moment in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point. Each creature of your choice in the Sphere must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d6 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. One creature of your choice in that area regains 2d6 Hit Points.

The damage and healing increase by 1d6 when you reach Druid levels 10 (3d6) and 14 (4d6).

properties.text: Magic action: expend one Wild Shape use. Creatures of your choice in a 10-ft radius Sphere at a point within 60 ft make a CON save (your spell save DC) — fail: 2d6 Necrotic damage, success: half. One creature of your choice in the area regains 2d6 HP. Scales to 3d6 at level 10, 4d6 at level 14.

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description:
You can cast one of the level 1+ spells that you have prepared from your Circle Spells feature without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a {rule:Long Rest} before you do so again.

In addition, when you finish a {rule:Short Rest}, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your Druid level (round up), and none of them can be level 6+. Once you recover spell slots with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: Once per Long Rest, cast one prepared Circle Spells spell (level 1+) without expending a slot. Also, once per Long Rest, after a Short Rest you can recover expended spell slots with combined level up to ceil(Druid level / 2), no slot level 6+.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: You are immune to the {condition:Poisoned} condition, and you have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to a damage type associated with your current land choice: Arid → Fire, Polar → Cold, Temperate → Lightning, Tropical → Poison.

properties.text: Gain resistance to a damage type based on current land choice: Arid = Fire, Polar = Cold, Temperate = Lightning, Tropical = Poison. Resistance changes when you choose a new land type on a Long Rest.

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description:
As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and cause spectral trees and vines to appear in a 15-foot Cube on the ground within 120 feet of yourself. They last there for 1 minute or until you have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition or die. You and your allies have Half Cover while in that area, and your allies gain the current resistance of your Nature's Ward while there.

As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can move the Cube up to 60 feet to ground within 120 feet of yourself.

properties.text: Magic action: expend one Wild Shape use to place a 15-ft Cube of spectral trees (within 120 ft, lasts 1 minute). You and allies in the area gain Half Cover. Allies also gain the current Nature's Ward resistance. Bonus Action: move the Cube up to 60 ft (must remain within 120 ft of you).

subclasses.name: Circle of the Moon
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Moon draw on lunar magic to transform themselves. Their order gathers under the moon to share news and perform rituals. Changeable as the moon, a Druid of this circle might prowl as a great cat one night, soar over the treetops as an eagle the next day, and then crash through undergrowth as a bear to drive off a trespassing monster.

features.name: Circle of the Moon Spells
features.description:
When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Moon Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. In addition, you can cast the spells from this feature while you're in a Wild Shape form.

Level 3: {spell:Cure Wounds}, {spell:Moonbeam}, {spell:Starry Wisp}; Level 5: {spell:Conjure Animals}; Level 7: {spell:Fount of Moonlight}; Level 9: {spell:Mass Cure Wounds}.

properties.text: All Circle of the Moon spells can be cast while in Wild Shape form.

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description:
You can channel lunar magic when you assume a Wild Shape form, granting you the following benefits.

**Challenge Rating.** The maximum Challenge Rating for the form equals your Druid level divided by 3 (round down).

**Armor Class.** Until you leave the form, your AC equals 13 plus your Wisdom modifier if that total is higher than the Beast's AC.

**Temporary Hit Points.** You gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to three times your Druid level.

properties.text: Wild Shape CR limit = Druid level / 3 (round down). While in Wild Shape, AC = max(Beast AC, 13 + Wis modifier). Temporary Hit Points when Wild Shaping = 3 × Druid level (overrides base Wild Shape temp HP rule of 1× Druid level).

features.name: Improved Circle Forms
features.description:
While in a Wild Shape form, you gain the following benefits.

**Lunar Radiance.** Each of your attacks in a Wild Shape form can deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. You make this choice each time you hit with those attacks.

**Increased Toughness.** You can add your Wisdom modifier to your Constitution saving throws.

properties.text: Wild Shape attacks can deal their normal damage type or Radiant damage (choose per hit). Also, add Wisdom modifier to Constitution saving throws (applies always, not only in Wild Shape).

features.name: Moonlight Step
features.description:
You magically transport yourself, reappearing amid a burst of moonlight. As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, and you have {rule:Advantage} on the next attack roll you make before the end of this turn.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}. You can also regain uses by expending a level 2+ spell slot for each use you want to restore (no action required).

properties.text: Bonus Action: teleport up to 30 ft to a visible unoccupied space; gain advantage on the next attack roll before end of this turn. Can also restore uses by expending a level 2+ spell slot per use (no action required).

features.name: Lunar Form
features.description:
The power of the moon suffuses you, granting you the following benefits.

**Improved Lunar Radiance.** Once per turn, you can deal an extra 2d10 Radiant damage to a target you hit with a Wild Shape form's attack.

**Shared Moonlight.** Whenever you use Moonlight Step, you can also teleport one willing creature. That creature must be within 10 feet of you, and you teleport it to an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of your destination space.

properties.text: Once per turn, Wild Shape form attacks can deal extra 2d10 Radiant damage. When using Moonlight Step, optionally also teleport one willing creature within 10 ft of you to within 10 ft of your destination.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Sea
subclasses.description: Druids of the Circle of the Sea draw on the tempestuous forces of oceans and storms. Some view themselves as embodiments of nature's wrath, seeking vengeance against those who despoil nature. Others seek mystical unity with nature by attuning themselves to the ebb and flow of the tides, following the rush of currents and waves and listening to the inscrutable whispers and roars of the winds.

features.name: Circle of the Sea Spells
features.description:
When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Sea Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Level 3: {spell:Fog Cloud}, {spell:Gust of Wind}, {spell:Ray of Frost}, {spell:Shatter}, {spell:Thunderwave}; Level 5: {spell:Lightning Bolt}, {spell:Water Breathing}; Level 7: {spell:Control Water}, {spell:Ice Storm}; Level 9: {spell:Conjure Elemental}, {spell:Hold Monster}.

features.name: Wrath of the Sea
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to manifest a 5-foot Emanation that takes the form of ocean spray surrounding you for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), manifest it again, or have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition.

When you manifest the Emanation and as a {rule:Bonus Action} on your subsequent turns, you can choose another creature you can see in the Emanation. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take Cold damage and, if the creature is Large or smaller, be pushed up to 15 feet away from you. To determine this damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of one die).

properties.text: Bonus Action: expend one Wild Shape use to surround yourself with a 5-ft ocean spray Emanation for 10 minutes. Each turn (Bonus Action), target one creature in the Emanation: CON save vs spell save DC or take Cold damage (Wis modifier in d6s, min 1d6) and be pushed 15 ft if Large or smaller.

features.name: Aquatic Affinity
features.description: The size of the Emanation created by your Wrath of the Sea increases to 10 feet. In addition, you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

properties.text: Wrath of the Sea Emanation radius increases to 10 feet. Swim Speed equals your Speed.

features.name: Stormborn
features.description:
Your Wrath of the Sea confers two more benefits while active.

**Flight.** You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.

**Resistance.** You have {rule:Resistance|resistance} to Cold, Lightning, and Thunder damage.

features.name: Oceanic Gift
features.description:
Instead of manifesting the Emanation of Wrath of the Sea around yourself, you can manifest it around one willing creature within 60 feet of yourself. That creature gains all the benefits of the Emanation and uses your spell save DC and Wisdom modifier for it.

In addition, you can manifest the Emanation around both the other creature and yourself if you expend two uses of your Wild Shape instead of one when manifesting it.

properties.text: Wrath of the Sea Emanation can be placed on a willing creature within 60 ft instead of yourself (uses your spell save DC and Wisdom modifier). Expend 2 Wild Shape uses to manifest it on both yourself and another creature simultaneously.

subclasses.name: Circle of the Stars
subclasses.description: The Circle of the Stars has tracked heavenly patterns since time immemorial, discovering secrets hidden amid the constellations. By understanding these secrets, the Druids of this circle seek to harness the powers of the cosmos.

features.name: Star Map
features.description:
You've created a star chart as part of your heavenly studies. It is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a {rule:Spellcasting Focus} for your Druid spells.

While holding the map, you have the {spell:Guidance} and {spell:Guiding Bolt} spells prepared, and you can cast {spell:Guiding Bolt} without expending a spell slot. You can cast it in that way a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

If you lose the map, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to magically create a replacement. This ceremony can be performed during a Short or Long Rest, and it destroys the previous map.

properties.text: While holding the Star Map, Guidance and Guiding Bolt are always prepared. You can cast Guiding Bolt without a spell slot a number of times equal to Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), regaining all uses on Long Rest. Map can be recreated in a 1-hour ceremony (Short or Long Rest).

features.name: Starry Form
features.description:
As a {rule:Bonus Action}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a starry form rather than shape-shifting. While in your starry form, you retain your game statistics, but your body becomes luminous; your joints glimmer like stars, and glowing lines connect them as on a star chart. This form sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. The form lasts for 10 minutes. It ends early if you dismiss it (no action required), have the {condition:Incapacitated} condition, or use this feature again.

Whenever you assume your starry form, choose which of the following constellations glimmers on your body.

**Archer.** A constellation of an archer appears on you. When you activate this form and as a {rule:Bonus Action} on your subsequent turns while it lasts, you can make a ranged spell attack, hurling a luminous arrow that targets one creature within 60 feet of yourself. On a hit, the attack deals Radiant damage equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier.

**Chalice.** A constellation of a life-giving goblet appears on you. Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores Hit Points to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet of you can regain Hit Points equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier.

**Dragon.** A constellation of a wise dragon appears on you. When you make an Intelligence or a Wisdom check or a Constitution saving throw to maintain {status:Concentration}, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

properties.text: Bonus Action: expend one Wild Shape use to enter Starry Form (10 min; sheds bright light 10 ft, dim light 10 ft more). Choose constellation: Archer (Bonus Action ranged spell attack, 1d8 + Wis Radiant on hit), Chalice (heal self or ally within 30 ft for 1d8 + Wis when casting a healing spell slot), or Dragon (treat d20 rolls of 9 or lower as 10 for Intelligence/Wisdom checks and CON saves to maintain Concentration).

features.name: Cosmic Omen
features.description:
Whenever you finish a {rule:Long Rest}, you can consult your Star Map for omens and roll a die. Until you finish your next {rule:Long Rest}, you gain access to a special {rule:Reaction} based on whether you rolled an even or an odd number on the die.

**Weal (even).** Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make a D20 Test, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d6 and add the number rolled to the total.

**Woe (odd).** Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make a D20 Test, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d6 and subtract the number rolled from the total.

You can use this Reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {rule:Long Rest}.

properties.text: After each Long Rest, roll a die: even = Weal (Reaction to add 1d6 to a D20 Test by a creature you can see within 30 ft), odd = Woe (Reaction to subtract 1d6 from such a test). Uses per Long Rest = Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

features.name: Twinkling Constellations
features.description:
The constellations of your Starry Form improve. The 1d8 of the Archer and the Chalice becomes 2d8, and while the Dragon is active, you have a Fly Speed of 20 feet and can hover.

Moreover, at the start of each of your turns while in your Starry Form, you can change which constellation glimmers on your body.

properties.text: Starry Form improvements: Archer and Chalice dice increase from 1d8 to 2d8. Dragon constellation grants a Fly Speed of 20 ft with hover. At the start of each turn in Starry Form, you can switch your active constellation.

features.name: Full of Stars
features.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you {rule:Resistance|resistance} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
